stemHtml: You are cruising at FL250, maintaining a constant CAS of 300 kt. The indicated Mach number is 0.7, with a static air temperature (SAT) of -35°C. If the SAT increases to -30°C and all other variables remain the same, the Mach number will…

options.a: increase because Mach number is a function of the local speed of sound, which increases with ambient temperature.
options.b: decrease but, after some time, increases once again because of increasing TAT.
options.c: remain constant because at a constant CAS and pressure altitude, Mach is independent of temperature.
options.d: decrease because LSS will increase with increases in temperature.
